title: Job Vacancies at RP Facilities Limited
content:
RP Facilities Limited is a Facility Management company with head office in Lagos, Nigeria with over a decade of experience that provides a variety of facility management services and Reverse Osmosis Water Treatment solutions. Our clients include commercial buildings, healthcare facilities, schools, and government buildings.

We are recruiting to fill the following positions below:

1.) Facility Manager

Job Location: Lekki  Phase 1, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Role Overview
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	We are seeking an experienced and proactive Facility Manager to be the cornerstone of our organization. In this role, you will harmonize our facilities management strategy with daily operational processes, ensuring that our team is empowered and our operations are efficient, scalable, and aligned with organizational goals.


Key Responsibilities


	Facilities Management: Oversee the maintenance, security, and functionality of all facilities, ensuring a safe and efficient working environment.
	Operational Efficiency: Streamline workflows and coordinate activities across departments to enhance overall productivity and minimize waste.
	Policy & Compliance: Develop and implement facility-related policies while ensuring strict adherence to local regulations and safety standards.
	Strategic Planning: Collaborate with leadership to forecast facility needs and translate business objectives into actionable operational plans.
	Resource Management: Manage vendor relationships, operational budgets, and facility resources to ensure cost-effectiveness and optimal performance.


Qualifications


	Interested candidates should possess a Bachelor's Degree with 5–8 years of progressive experience in facilities management or operations, with at least 5 years in a supervisory role.


Required Skills:


	Leadership: Proven ability to lead and inspire teams.
	Tech-Savviness: Proficient in facility management software and technology.
	Analytical Thinking: Strong ability to analyze data and make informed decisions.
	Communication: Excellent verbal and written communication skills.
	Problem-Solving: Effective at identifying issues and implementing solutions.


Application Closing Date: 11th April, 2026.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV and a brief Cover Letter to: [email protected] using "Facility Manager" as the subject of the email.

 

 

2.) Plumber

Job Location: Lagos 
Employment Type: Full-time

Responsibilities


	Water Treatment
	Sewage Treatment
	Building Plumbing Maintenance.


Application Closing Date: 11th April, 2026.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV and a brief Cover Letter to: [email protected] using "Plumbing" as the subject of the mail.

 

 

3.) HR and Operations Manager

Job Location: Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Role Overview


	We are seeking a versatile and proactive HR & Operations Manager to serve as the backbone of our organization.
	You will be responsible for harmonizing our people strategy with our daily business processes, ensuring that our team is empowered and our operations are lean, efficient, and scalable.


Key Responsibilities


	Human Resources: Lead the full employee lifecycle, including recruitment, onboarding, performance management, and offboarding.
	Operational Oversight: Streamline workflows and coordinate across departments to improve overall business productivity and reduce waste.
	Policy & Compliance: Develop and implement company policies while ensuring 100% adherence to local labor laws and safety regulations.
	Strategic Planning: Assist leadership in forecasting staffing needs and translating business goals into actionable operational plans.
	Resource Management: Manage office facilities, vendor relationships, and operational budgets to ensure cost-effectiveness.


Experience


	Experience: 5–8 years of progressive experience in HR and/or Operations, with at least 2 years in a supervisory role.


Required Skills:


	Leadership
	Tech-Savviness.
	Analytical Thinking
	Communication.
	Problem-Solving.


Application Closing Date: 22nd March, 2026.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should submit their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position  as the subject of the email.

     

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Billing Support Engineer",
  "description": "VDT Communications Limited is Nigeria&#39;s broadband communication service leader. We offer a complete turnkey solution to all your data communication needs. VDT hasseveral years of experience in providing data communication services to corporate organizations with many branches across Nigeria. We seek to provide a reliable, end-to-end source for telecommunications services with a broad product suite backed by trustworthy, dependable customer service.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Billing Support Engineer

Job Location:&nbsp;Ikoyi, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Description


	We are&nbsp;looking to hire a Billing Support Engineer to support the management, optimization, and integrity of our billing systems and platforms.
	This role is ideal for a technically strong professional with experience in telecom/ISP billing systems, BSS environments, and service provisioning workflows.


Role Responsibilities


	Support day-to-day operations of VDT&rsquo;s Billing/BSS platforms and related infrastructure.
	Monitor, analyze, and troubleshoot billing logs, transaction records, and system events.
	Investigate and resolve billing discrepancies, invoice disputes, and revenue-related issues.
	Support User Acceptance Testing (UAT), system upgrades, and go-live activities for billing implementations.
	Assist in the integration of third-party applications, network elements, and service provisioning systems into the billing platform.
	Generate and automate operational, financial, and performance reports for Finance, Sales, Customer Care, and Technical teams.
	Provide technical support for data plan configuration, product setup, and billing policies.
	Maintain proper technical documentation, processes, and system records.


Required Qualifications


	Bachelor&rsquo;s Degree in Electrical / Electronics Engineering, Computer Science, Systems Engineering, or related field.
	3&ndash;6 years of experience supporting Billing / BSS platforms within a telecommunications or ISP environment.
	Strong knowledge of billing architectures, service provisioning workflows, and revenue assurance processes.
	Experience analyzing transaction logs, system reports, and database records.
	SQL/database knowledge is an added advantage.
	Relevant certifications such as CCNA, MCSA, MCSE, or similar are a plus.


Key Competencies:


	Strong analytical and troubleshooting skills
	High attention to detail and data accuracy
	Ability to work with minimal supervision
	Effective collaboration across technical and commercial teams


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to:&nbsp;recruitment@vdtcomms.com&nbsp;using &quot;Billing Support Engineer&quot;&nbsp;as the subject of the mail.",

Billing Support Engineer Job at VDT Communications Limited




        
    

        
        VDT Communications Limited is Nigeria's broadband communication service leader. We offer a complete turnkey solution to all your data communication needs. VDT hasseveral years of experience in providing data communication services to corporate organizations with many branches across Nigeria. We seek to provide a reliable, end-to-end source for telecommunications services with a broad product suite backed by trustworthy, dependable customer service.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Billing Support Engineer

Job Location: Ikoyi, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Description
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	We are looking to hire a Billing Support Engineer to support the management, optimization, and integrity of our billing systems and platforms.
	This role is ideal for a technically strong professional with experience in telecom/ISP billing systems, BSS environments, and service provisioning workflows.


Role Responsibilities


	Support day-to-day operations of VDT’s Billing/BSS platforms and related infrastructure.
	Monitor, analyze, and troubleshoot billing logs, transaction records, and system events.
	Investigate and resolve billing discrepancies, invoice disputes, and revenue-related issues.
	Support User Acceptance Testing (UAT), system upgrades, and go-live activities for billing implementations.
	Assist in the integration of third-party applications, network elements, and service provisioning systems into the billing platform.
	Generate and automate operational, financial, and performance reports for Finance, Sales, Customer Care, and Technical teams.
	Provide technical support for data plan configuration, product setup, and billing policies.
	Maintain proper technical documentation, processes, and system records.


Required Qualifications


	Bachelor’s Degree in Electrical / Electronics Engineering, Computer Science, Systems Engineering, or related field.
	3–6 years of experience supporting Billing / BSS platforms within a telecommunications or ISP environment.
	Strong knowledge of billing architectures, service provisioning workflows, and revenue assurance processes.
	Experience analyzing transaction logs, system reports, and database records.
	SQL/database knowledge is an added advantage.
	Relevant certifications such as CCNA, MCSA, MCSE, or similar are a plus.


Key Competencies:


	Strong analytical and troubleshooting skills
	High attention to detail and data accuracy
	Ability to work with minimal supervision
	Effective collaboration across technical and commercial teams


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using "Billing Support Engineer" as the subject of the mail.    

    

        Click here to Apply

  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Customer Service Officer / Teller",
  "description": "MADOBI Microfinance Bank (MFB) Limited is&nbsp;recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Customer Service Officer / Teller
Job Location:&nbsp;Kano

Summary


	The Customer Service Officer / Teller serves as the bank&rsquo;s frontline ambassador, responsible for delivering exceptional customer service, accurately processing transactions, and enforcing robust internal controls and compliance standards.
	The role integrates cash handling, savings mobilization, customer engagement, product promotion, and risk oversight to support financial inclusion, operational efficiency, and regulatory compliance (CBN, AML/CFT, and BOFIA).


Responsibilities


	Deliver prompt and courteous service: handle inquiries, complaints, account openings/updates, and product education for savings and small loans.
	Process high-volume small transactions: deposits, withdrawals, transfers, loan repayments, and bill payments.
	Maintain and reconcile cash drawers/vaults daily with zero tolerance for discrepancies.
	Apply strong internal controls: verify KYC/BVN, monitor suspicious activities, enforce limits, and use dual controls.
	Conduct transaction reviews and spot checks to prevent fraud and ensure audit readiness.
	Promote savings products, small loans, and other services while achieving cross-selling and referral targets.


Requirements


	Bachelor&rsquo;s Degree in Accounting, Finance, Banking, Business Administration, or related field.
	Minimum of 2 years of experience in internal audits, controls, or compliance in banking or financial services (essential).
	Solid knowledge of CBN regulations, AML/CFT, cash management, and internal controls.
	Proficiency in core banking systems and Microsoft Office (Excel)
	Excellent communication, interpersonal, and problem-solving skills with high integrity.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should forward their updated CV as a PDF file to:&nbsp;recruitments@istrategytech.com&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.",

Customer Service Officer / Teller Job at MADOBI Microfinance Bank




        
    

        
        MADOBI Microfinance Bank (MFB) Limited is recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Customer Service Officer / Teller
Job Location: Kano

Summary
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	The Customer Service Officer / Teller serves as the bank’s frontline ambassador, responsible for delivering exceptional customer service, accurately processing transactions, and enforcing robust internal controls and compliance standards.
	The role integrates cash handling, savings mobilization, customer engagement, product promotion, and risk oversight to support financial inclusion, operational efficiency, and regulatory compliance (CBN, AML/CFT, and BOFIA).


Responsibilities


	Deliver prompt and courteous service: handle inquiries, complaints, account openings/updates, and product education for savings and small loans.
	Process high-volume small transactions: deposits, withdrawals, transfers, loan repayments, and bill payments.
	Maintain and reconcile cash drawers/vaults daily with zero tolerance for discrepancies.
	Apply strong internal controls: verify KYC/BVN, monitor suspicious activities, enforce limits, and use dual controls.
	Conduct transaction reviews and spot checks to prevent fraud and ensure audit readiness.
	Promote savings products, small loans, and other services while achieving cross-selling and referral targets.


Requirements


	Bachelor’s Degree in Accounting, Finance, Banking, Business Administration, or related field.
	Minimum of 2 years of experience in internal audits, controls, or compliance in banking or financial services (essential).
	Solid knowledge of CBN regulations, AML/CFT, cash management, and internal controls.
	Proficiency in core banking systems and Microsoft Office (Excel)
	Excellent communication, interpersonal, and problem-solving skills with high integrity.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should forward their updated CV as a PDF file to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Manager, Relationship - Commercial Banking",
  "description": "Stanbic IBTC Bank is a leading African banking group focused on emerging markets globally. It has been a mainstay of South Africa&#39;s financial system for 150 years, and now spans 16 countries across the African continent.

Standard Bank is a firm believer in technical innovation, to help us guarantee exceptional client service and leading edge financial solutions. Our growing global success reflects our commitment to the latest solutions, the best people, and a uniquely flexible and vibrant working culture. To help us drive our success into the future, we are looking for resourceful individuals to join our dedicated team at our offices.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position:&nbsp;Manager, Relationship - Commercial Banking

Job Ref ID:&nbsp;80450235A-0001
Job Location:&nbsp;Maiduguri, Borno

Job Description


	A Relationship Manager is accountable for a portfolio of Commercial Banking Clients. Commercial Banking Segment is for Companies who carry out a turnover of at least N2billion for Tier 2 and at Least N5bn Turnover annually for Tier 1.
	It is expected that the RM integrates and coordinates all Stanbic IBTC and Standard Bank Group products, services and resources in order to maximize the value of the business relationship and ensure profitability for the Bank and the Client.


Qualifications


	First Degree in any field
	Minimum of Relationship management experience of at least 1-2&nbsp;years in Corporate Banking
	Minimum of Relationship management experience of at least 1-2&nbsp;years in Finance
	Minimum of Relationship management experience of at least 7-10 years in a commercial Bank
	Cognate experience in sales and relationship management of customers to be able to drive profitability and build relationships.


Behavioural Competencies:


	Generating Ideas
	Examining Information
	Thinking Positively
	Taking Action
	Seizing Opportunities.


Technical Competencies:


	Customer Understanding
	Product Knowledge
	Application and Submission Verification
	Customer Acceptance and Review
	Product Related Systems.
",

Manager, Relationship - Commercial Banking Job at Stanbic IBTC Bank




        
    

        
        Stanbic IBTC Bank is a leading African banking group focused on emerging markets globally. It has been a mainstay of South Africa's financial system for 150 years, and now spans 16 countries across the African continent.

Standard Bank is a firm believer in technical innovation, to help us guarantee exceptional client service and leading edge financial solutions. Our growing global success reflects our commitment to the latest solutions, the best people, and a uniquely flexible and vibrant working culture. To help us drive our success into the future, we are looking for resourceful individuals to join our dedicated team at our offices.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    



Job Position: Manager, Relationship - Commercial Banking

Job Ref ID: 80450235A-0001
Job Location: Maiduguri, Borno

Job Description


	A Relationship Manager is accountable for a portfolio of Commercial Banking Clients. Commercial Banking Segment is for Companies who carry out a turnover of at least N2billion for Tier 2 and at Least N5bn Turnover annually for Tier 1.
	It is expected that the RM integrates and coordinates all Stanbic IBTC and Standard Bank Group products, services and resources in order to maximize the value of the business relationship and ensure profitability for the Bank and the Client.


Qualifications


	First Degree in any field
	Minimum of Relationship management experience of at least 1-2 years in Corporate Banking
	Minimum of Relationship management experience of at least 1-2 years in Finance
	Minimum of Relationship management experience of at least 7-10 years in a commercial Bank
	Cognate experience in sales and relationship management of customers to be able to drive profitability and build relationships.


Behavioural Competencies:


	Generating Ideas
	Examining Information
	Thinking Positively
	Taking Action
	Seizing Opportunities.


Technical Competencies:


	Customer Understanding
	Product Knowledge
	Application and Submission Verification
	Customer Acceptance and Review
	Product Related Systems.

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Sales Representative",
  "description": "Bedmate Furniture, Nigeria&#39;s leading furniture company with a rich history spanning over two decades. Since our establishment in 2002, we have been dedicated to providing exceptional furniture solutions to homes, offices, and commercial spaces across the country.&nbsp;As a socially responsible company, we strive to make a positive impact on our community and the environment. We adhere to sustainable practices, utilizing eco-friendly materials and minimizing waste in our production processes. Additionally, we actively engage in philanthropic initiatives, supporting local charities and organizations that promote education, healthcare, and social welfare.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Sales Representative

Job Location:&nbsp;Wuye, Wuse - Abuja (FCT)
Employment Type: Full-time

Responsibilities


	Ensure every pre-assigned sales target is met each month.
	Approach customers as they enter the showroom, inquire about the type of furniture they want, make suggestions and show them around
	Manage the sales process and close every sale with potential customers.
	Politely respond to questions/inquires raised by customers, conduct suggestive selling so as to move multiple products, process the paperwork and arrange financing when needed.
	Maintain accurate and up to date records of customers who do not make a purchase and follow up as needed.
	Maintain a clean and organized appearance of the showroom by regularly dusting and arranging displays, putting up any point-of-sale information and tags accurately
	Carry out physical inventory counts and assisting with orders.
	Constantly learn about new products and be able to explain specific features of various products to customers.
	Ensure maximum focus is given to excellent service and customer satisfaction.
	Process quotes and sales orders
	Assist in showroom set-up, design, and presentation of products
	Convert visitors into lifelong clients by being able to deliver timely follow-up, provide quality service, and ensure client satisfaction
	Explain different customizable features to customers and show them how to maximize their features.
	Discuss warranties and replacement of parts.
	Notify installers after closing a sales deal to collapse and package purchases.
	Represent and promote the organization&rsquo;s products and services at events such as sales parties
	Conduct outbound lead follow-up calls to potential and existing customers via telephone and e-mail to sell products and services.
	Ensure pricing is correct and accurate.
	Notify sales supervisor of any after sales issues with customers
	Any other duties as assigned by ManagemenT


Requirements


	Candidates should possess an OND, HND, or First Degree in Business Administration, Marketing, Finance, Social Sciences or any other relevant discipline is required.
	Minimum of 2 years of successful experience in sales
	Knowledge of commercial interiors and office furniture are considered a plus.


Salary
N80,000 - N90,000 / Month.

Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should forward their Cover Letter and CV in one document to:&nbsp;recruitmentbedmatefurniture@gmail.com&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the email.

Note:&nbsp;Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted for a physical interview.",
  "datePosted": "2026-03-10",
  "validThrough": "2026-04-12",
  "employmentType": "Full-Time",
  "hiringOrganization": {
    "@type": "Organization",
    "name": "Bedmate Furniture Nigeria"
    
  },
  "jobLocation": {
    "@type": "Place",
    "address": {
      "@type": "PostalAddress",
       "postalCode": ". ",
       "addressRegion": ". ",

      "addressCountry": "NG"

Sales Representative Job at Bedmate Furniture




        
    

        
        Bedmate Furniture, Nigeria's leading furniture company with a rich history spanning over two decades. Since our establishment in 2002, we have been dedicated to providing exceptional furniture solutions to homes, offices, and commercial spaces across the country. As a socially responsible company, we strive to make a positive impact on our community and the environment. We adhere to sustainable practices, utilizing eco-friendly materials and minimizing waste in our production processes. Additionally, we actively engage in philanthropic initiatives, supporting local charities and organizations that promote education, healthcare, and social welfare.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Sales Representative
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    



Job Location: Wuye, Wuse - Abuja (FCT)
Employment Type: Full-time

Responsibilities


	Ensure every pre-assigned sales target is met each month.
	Approach customers as they enter the showroom, inquire about the type of furniture they want, make suggestions and show them around
	Manage the sales process and close every sale with potential customers.
	Politely respond to questions/inquires raised by customers, conduct suggestive selling so as to move multiple products, process the paperwork and arrange financing when needed.
	Maintain accurate and up to date records of customers who do not make a purchase and follow up as needed.
	Maintain a clean and organized appearance of the showroom by regularly dusting and arranging displays, putting up any point-of-sale information and tags accurately
	Carry out physical inventory counts and assisting with orders.
	Constantly learn about new products and be able to explain specific features of various products to customers.
	Ensure maximum focus is given to excellent service and customer satisfaction.
	Process quotes and sales orders
	Assist in showroom set-up, design, and presentation of products
	Convert visitors into lifelong clients by being able to deliver timely follow-up, provide quality service, and ensure client satisfaction
	Explain different customizable features to customers and show them how to maximize their features.
	Discuss warranties and replacement of parts.
	Notify installers after closing a sales deal to collapse and package purchases.
	Represent and promote the organization’s products and services at events such as sales parties
	Conduct outbound lead follow-up calls to potential and existing customers via telephone and e-mail to sell products and services.
	Ensure pricing is correct and accurate.
	Notify sales supervisor of any after sales issues with customers
	Any other duties as assigned by ManagemenT


Requirements


	Candidates should possess an OND, HND, or First Degree in Business Administration, Marketing, Finance, Social Sciences or any other relevant discipline is required.
	Minimum of 2 years of successful experience in sales
	Knowledge of commercial interiors and office furniture are considered a plus.


Salary
N80,000 - N90,000 / Month.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should forward their Cover Letter and CV in one document to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.

Note: Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted for a physical interview.    

    

        Click here to Apply
